rationale: Targeted the two largest wrong_result classes from SQLWriter — datetime equality/evidence-polarity and superlative-over-versioned-grain — via memory pitfalls plus a validator strategy that forces an executed SELECT and interpretation-picking on typo/ambiguous questions (the no_sql_executed class). These deterministic procedures replace judgment calls and should lift both A_mean and U90 by ensuring runnable, correctly-grained queries.

prompt_patches.new_text: Your job is to guarantee the run ends with ONE executed, plausible SELECT. Never allow the turn to end with a clarification question, a schema/table dump, or empty SQL ATTEMPTS. If the question is typo-corrupted or ambiguous, do NOT bounce for clarification: instruct the writer to pick the single most likely interpretation (e.g. 'code' -> drivers.code; repair a garbled token to the sample_rows value it most resembles, like a races.name) and emit SQL noting the assumption. Always execute at least one query. If the result is empty or implausible (zero rows on a filter that should match, a count far off scale), retry ONCE: re-check datetime/enum literal formats and predicate polarity against the evidence note, then accept. When bouncing to the writer, include the exact error message or a one-sentence diagnosis — never a bare bounce. A validated executed SELECT MUST exist before handoff to Summarizer.

memory_upserts.text: DATETIME columns store trailing fractions ('2010-07-19 19:15:52.0'); never filter with = 'HH:MM:SS on YYYY/M/D' as a plain literal — use LIKE '2013-07-12 09:08:18%' or a range, or exact equality returns zero rows. For yes/no 'well-finished/closed/open' questions, map the boolean to the polarity the evidence note states; do NOT assume ClosedDate IS NOT NULL means the positive answer — read the evidence.

When a table has multiple time-versioned rows per entity (e.g. Player_Attributes: many dated rows per player_api_id), do NOT apply WHERE col=(SELECT MAX(col) FROM t) over all snapshots — that counts attribute rows, not entities. First aggregate/reduce to entity grain (per-player latest or per-player MAX), then apply the superlative. Use WHERE col=(SELECT MAX...) only when the table is one-row-per-entity. COUNT DISTINCT the entity id.

For any ratio/percentage/average-per-unit, CAST the numerator AS REAL before dividing: CAST(SUM(x) AS REAL)/SUM(y). Integer/integer truncates to 0. Re-read the evidence note to confirm the exact numerator and denominator definition (e.g. avg price per item = SUM(Price)/SUM(Amount) only if evidence says so) before emitting the expression.

When the question names a categorical value (a publisher name, gender, a 'weekly' frequency), find its coded literal in the lookup table via sample_rows and pass the exact mapping to the writer (account.frequency='POPLATEK TYDNE'=weekly; gender='F'=female). Enumerate EVERY join needed: multiple attribute columns each need their own lookup self-join (eye/hair/skin colour = three separate colour joins). Do not drop requested filter predicates.
